title: fix: keep framework caches out of persisted review state
author: maciej-trebacz

stage1.summary: This PR addresses the ROOT CAUSE of the dataclass serialization crash that commit 61bb7cb3 only mitigated defensively. The code path is clear: workflow.py passes a reference to state["review_cache"] into LangRun, then framework detection (detection.py) and nextjs info (nextjs.py) write dataclass objects (EcosystemFrameworkDetection, NextjsFrameworkInfo) into that shared dict. When save_state serializes to JSON, these non-serializable objects cause a crash. The fix correctly introduces a separate runtime_cache field that is never persisted, cleanly separating ephemeral scan-scoped memoization from persisted review data. The implementation is thorough: it adds runtime_cache to LangRuntimeState, LangRunOverrides, LangRuntimeContract protocol, accessors, and the override dict fields set. Framework caching in both detection.py and nextjs.py is moved to runtime_cache. Tests cover runtime isolation, accessor round-trips, and an integration test proving framework objects stay out of persisted state.
